headline: The political left is disappearing across Central Europe, despite Orban's defeat in Hungary
description: Social democrats no longer have any MPs in Hungary, Bulgaria or the Czech Republic; have been pushed into opposition in Slovenia; and are polling at less than 10% in Poland. Often originating from former communist parties, they first lost working-class voters to more nationalist groups, and then, in the 2010s, lost the middle class.

Two off-duty riot police officers prosecuted for assaulting man in Nice
description: The two officers, who appear to be drunk on footage of the scene posted online, assaulted a man during the night of April 18. They will stand trial in June after partly admitting to the charges.

headline: Ban on toxic chemicals stalled by EU's 'limited progress'
description: The European Environmental Bureau and ClientEarth have considered the number of cases piling up on the European Commission's desk to be 'illegal and unacceptable' in a new report.

headline: Les Flammes 2026: Theodora triumphs as Gims and Werenoi also honored
description: More polished and better organized, Thursday's ceremony for French popular music crowned Theodora as Best Female Artist. Gims took home the award for Best Male Artist, while Hamza won Best Song of the Year.

headline: 'How can anyone love Céline Dion? The hysteria surrounding her Paris concerts is astonishing'
description: After the spectacular sale of nearly 500,000 tickets for a series of shows in Paris this autumn, Le Monde columnist Michel Guerrin questions in his column what drives people to love (or hate) the singer.

Boris Pistorius, the man preparing Germany for war
description: The country's most popular politician for the past three years, who started serving as defense minister under Olaf Scholz, is set to unveil a military strategy review on Wednesday aimed at making the Bundeswehr the leading conventional army in Europe. Despite receiving unprecedented financial resources, the institution has been slow to reform.

headline: 'AI faces a growing backlash from the American public' due to its energy consumption and impact on jobs
description: In the United States, protests from both Democrats and Republicans have risen against planned data centers required for artificial intelligence, explains Le Monde's Alexandre Piquard in his column.

headline: Are language disorders on the rise in France? Concern grows in the era of screens
description: Testing among young children has expanded significantly in recent years, but there is a lack of studies to assess their prevalence.
